data.title: Enriched task descriptions: requires_visual_verification metadata
data.description:
## User Story
As a sprint executor, when a task modifies template/CSS/JS/component files, I want `requires_visual_verification: true` metadata in the task description with Playwright AC so visual regressions are caught.

## Done Definitions
1. When generating task descriptions, implementation-plan detects if any file in the task's file impact list matches template/CSS/JS/component patterns (.css, .js, .ts, .tsx, .html, .jinja2, .erb, component files)
2. When detected, task description includes `requires_visual_verification: true` metadata field
3. Task description includes Playwright AC specifying what visual behavior to verify (e.g., "Run playwright test targeting the component; verify no visual regression against baseline")
4. The metadata and Playwright AC are instructions to the sub-agent executing the task — the sub-agent runs the Playwright tests as part of task execution; the sprint orchestrator does not need a separate dispatch step
5. When no UI files detected, metadata and Playwright AC are absent
6. Tests: structural boundary test validates metadata field format; integration test: given a task with a .css file, task description includes `requires_visual_verification: true` and Playwright AC

## Escalation Policy
Escalate when blocked: Agents proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
